US-2.1

Researcher Search & Filter

**As a** researcher, **I can** search and filter the researcher list by lab, theme, or keyword.

Acceptance Criteria

  • GIVEN the researcher list loads, WHEN the user types in the search field, THEN the table filters in real-time to show matching researchers by name or keyword.
  • GIVEN the researcher list is displayed, WHEN the user selects a lab from the dropdown, THEN only researchers from that lab are shown.
  • GIVEN the researcher list is displayed, WHEN the user selects a theme from the dropdown, THEN only researchers with that theme are shown.
  • GIVEN both filters are active, WHEN combined, THEN they apply with AND logic (both conditions must be true).

Happy Path

1
Action: Navigate to `/researchers`
Expected: Researcher list page loads with heading "Chercheurs"
2
Action: Observe the table
Expected: Table shows researchers with columns: Nom, Laboratoire, Themes, Publications, Actions
3
Action: Type "Dupont" in the search field
Expected: Table filters to show only Dr. Marie Dupont
4
Action: Clear search, select "LORIA" from lab dropdown
Expected: Table filters to show only researchers from LORIA
5
Action: Additionally select "Process Discovery" from theme dropdown
Expected: Table filters with AND logic (LORIA + Process Discovery)
6
Action: Click "Explorer par theme" cross-nav button
Expected: Navigates to `/themes`

Edge Case E1: No Results

GIVEN the user types a query matching no researchers, WHEN the table filters, THEN a "Aucun resultat" message is shown with suggestion to adjust filters.

1
Action: Navigate to `/researchers`
Expected: List loads
2
Action: Type "xyznonexistent" in the search field
Expected: No matches
3
Action: Observe the table
Expected: "Aucun resultat" message with suggestion to adjust filters

Edge Case E2: API Failure

GIVEN the researcher API fails, WHEN the list loads, THEN an error message replaces the table with a retry button.

1
Action: Intercept `/api/researchers` with `page.route('**/api/researchers**', r => r.abort())`
Expected: API blocked
2
Action: Navigate to `/researchers`
Expected: Page loads
3
Action: Observe the content area
Expected: Error message replaces table with retry button

Edge Case E3: Special Characters in Search

GIVEN a search query contains special characters (<, >, &), WHEN entered, THEN they are sanitized and do not break the display or cause XSS.

1
Action: Navigate to `/researchers`
Expected: List loads
2
Action: Type `<script>alert('xss')</script>` in the search field
Expected: Input is sanitized
3
Action: Observe the page
Expected: No XSS execution, search treats it as plain text, no results shown
US-2.2

Researcher Profile View

**As a** researcher, **I can** view a full profile (info, themes, publications, map position).

Acceptance Criteria

  • GIVEN a researcher is selected, WHEN their profile loads, THEN the sidebar displays avatar (initials), full name, lab, and bio text.
  • GIVEN the profile loads, WHEN keywords exist, THEN they display as colored tags in the keywords card.
  • GIVEN the profile loads, WHEN publications exist, THEN they display as a list with title, co-authors, and venue/year.
  • GIVEN the profile has map data, WHEN the user clicks "Voir sur la carte," THEN they navigate to the map centered on that researcher.

Happy Path

1
Action: Navigate to `/researchers`
Expected: List loads
2
Action: Click "Voir" on Dr. Marie Dupont's row
Expected: Navigates to profile page
3
Action: Observe the profile sidebar
Expected: Avatar with initials "MD", name "Dr. Marie Dupont", lab "LORIA", bio text
4
Action: Observe the keywords card
Expected: Tags displayed: Process Discovery, Conformance Checking, etc.
5
Action: Observe the publications list
Expected: Publications with title, co-authors, venue/year
6
Action: Verify breadcrumb
Expected: "Chercheurs > Profil" breadcrumb visible
7
Action: Click "Chercheurs" in breadcrumb
Expected: Navigates back to researcher list

Edge Case E1: No Publications

GIVEN a researcher has no publications, WHEN the profile loads, THEN the publications section shows "Aucune publication enregistree."

1
Action: Intercept researcher API to return a profile with empty publications array
Expected: Profile with no pubs
2
Action: Navigate to that profile
Expected: Profile loads
3
Action: Observe publications section
Expected: "Aucune publication enregistree" message

Edge Case E2: Profile 404

GIVEN the profile API returns 404, WHEN navigating to a profile, THEN a "Profil introuvable" message is shown with a link back to the list.

1
Action: Intercept `/api/researchers/:id` with `page.route('**/api/researchers/**', r => r.fulfill({ status: 404 }))`
Expected: 404 response
2
Action: Navigate to `/researchers/nonexistent-id`
Expected: Page loads
3
Action: Observe the content
Expected: "Profil introuvable" message with link back to list

Edge Case E3: Very Long Bio

GIVEN a researcher has a very long bio (>2000 chars), WHEN displayed, THEN it is truncated with a "Lire la suite" expand toggle.

1
Action: Intercept researcher API returning a bio with 3000 characters
Expected: Long bio profile
2
Action: Navigate to profile
Expected: Profile loads
3
Action: Observe the bio area
Expected: Bio is truncated with "Lire la suite" link
4
Action: Click "Lire la suite"
Expected: Full bio is revealed
US-2.4

Side-by-Side Comparison

**As a** researcher, **I can** compare two researchers side-by-side with common themes and similarity score.

Acceptance Criteria

  • GIVEN the comparison screen loads, WHEN two researchers are selected, THEN their profiles display in two columns with a similarity gauge in the center.
  • GIVEN the comparison displays, WHEN common themes exist, THEN they are visually highlighted with a blue outline on both sides.
  • GIVEN the comparison displays, WHEN the similarity score is computed, THEN it shows as a percentage inside a circular gauge.
  • GIVEN the comparison displays, WHEN common themes exist, THEN a summary card at the bottom lists shared themes with a textual explanation.

Happy Path

1
Action: Navigate to a researcher's profile
Expected: Profile loads
Missing: US-2.4-01-profile-loaded.png
2
Action: Click "Comparer" button
Expected: Navigates to comparison page
Missing: US-2.4-02-comparison-page.png
3
Action: Select a second researcher for comparison
Expected: Second researcher chosen
Missing: US-2.4-03-second-selected.png
4
Action: Observe the comparison layout
Expected: Two profiles in columns with similarity gauge in center
Missing: US-2.4-04-layout.png
5
Action: Observe the similarity gauge
Expected: Circular gauge shows percentage (e.g., 72%)
Missing: US-2.4-05-gauge.png
6
Action: Observe common themes
Expected: Shared themes highlighted with blue outline on both sides
Missing: US-2.4-06-common-themes.png
7
Action: Observe summary card
Expected: Summary section at bottom lists shared themes with explanation
Missing: US-2.4-07-summary.png

Edge Case E1: Zero Common Themes

GIVEN two researchers share zero themes, WHEN compared, THEN the gauge shows "0%" and the common themes section reads "Aucun theme commun."

1
Action: Intercept similarity API to return `{ score: 0, common_themes: [] }`
Expected: Zero similarity
2
Action: Load comparison page
Expected: Comparison renders
3
Action: Observe the gauge
Expected: Shows "0%"
4
Action: Observe common themes section
Expected: "Aucun theme commun" message

Edge Case E2: Similarity API Failure

GIVEN the similarity API fails, WHEN the comparison loads, THEN a fallback message "Score de similarite indisponible" replaces the gauge.

1
Action: Intercept similarity API with `page.route('**/api/similarity/**', r => r.fulfill({ status: 504 }))`
Expected: Timeout
2
Action: Load comparison page
Expected: Comparison renders
3
Action: Observe the gauge area
Expected: "Score de similarite indisponible" message

Edge Case E3: Same Researcher Twice

GIVEN the user selects the same researcher twice, WHEN comparison loads, THEN a message "Veuillez selectionner deux chercheurs differents" is shown.

1
Action: Navigate to comparison with same researcher ID for both
Expected: Same ID used twice
Missing: US-2.4-E3-01-same-id.png
2
Action: Observe the page
Expected: "Veuillez selectionner deux chercheurs differents" message
US-2.5

Profile to Map Navigation

**As a** researcher, **I can** navigate from a profile to that person's position on the thematic map.

Acceptance Criteria

  • GIVEN a profile is displayed, WHEN the user clicks "Voir sur la carte," THEN the map screen opens.
  • GIVEN the map opens from a profile, WHEN it renders, THEN the view is centered on the selected researcher's dot.
  • GIVEN the map centers on a researcher, WHEN it renders, THEN that researcher's dot pulses or is visually highlighted.

Happy Path

1
Action: Navigate to Dr. Marie Dupont's profile
Expected: Profile loads
2
Action: Locate the "Voir sur la carte" button in the sidebar
Expected: Button visible
3
Action: Click "Voir sur la carte"
Expected: Navigates to `/map`
4
Action: Observe the map
Expected: Map is centered on Marie Dupont's dot, which is highlighted/pulsing

Edge Case E1: No Map Coordinates

GIVEN a researcher has no map coordinates (new, unprocessed), WHEN "Voir sur la carte" is clicked, THEN a toast "Ce chercheur n'a pas encore de position sur la carte" appears and the button is disabled.

1
Action: Intercept researcher API returning null map_x and map_y
Expected: No coordinates
2
Action: Navigate to that profile
Expected: Profile loads
3
Action: Observe "Voir sur la carte" button
Expected: Button disabled or shows toast "Ce chercheur n'a pas encore de position sur la carte"

Edge Case E2: Map Data API Failure

GIVEN the map data API fails, WHEN navigating, THEN the map shows an error overlay with a retry button.

1
Action: Intercept `/api/clusters` with `page.route('**/api/clusters', r => r.abort())`
Expected: API blocked
2
Action: Click "Voir sur la carte" from a profile
Expected: Navigates to map
3
Action: Observe the map area
Expected: Error overlay with "Chargement echoue" and retry button

Edge Case E3: Cluster Reorganized

GIVEN the researcher belongs to a cluster that was recently reorganized, WHEN navigating, THEN the map still centers correctly using the latest coordinates.

1
Action: Intercept researcher API to return updated coordinates (different from original)
Expected: Updated positions
2
Action: Click "Voir sur la carte" from profile
Expected: Navigates to map
3
Action: Observe the map
Expected: Map correctly centers on the new coordinates

Known Limitations

  • Similarity is computed client-side via Jaccard on keywords — server-side NLP (TF-IDF/BERT) deferred to later milestone
  • "Voir sur la carte" button is disabled (not toast) when researcher has no map coordinates
  • Bio truncation uses character count, not word boundaries — may cut mid-word
  • D3.js bundle is 510KB in a single chunk — code-splitting deferred to M5

What's Next

  • **Interactive Thematic Map** — Full SVG cluster map with zoom/pan, click-to-see-members, and researcher dot navigation
  • **Theme Explorer** — Grid of expandable cluster cards showing themes and their member researchers
  • **Detailed Statistics** — Bar chart (theme distribution), line chart (temporal trends), histogram (similarity scores)
